rationale: The cited paper is not just listed in background citations; it is used in the citing paper's theorizing and measurement. Hoffman et al. cite Kopalle and Lehmann (2001) when defining optimism and explicitly adopt its three-item consumer optimism scale in Study 1. That scale is then used in their construct-validation analyses, so the citation plays a concrete methodological role in the empirical setup.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Kopalle and Lehmann (2001) by importing its consumer optimism construct and scale into a new-product concept development context. The borrowed measure is used as part of validating the emergent nature framework.
